title: The standing ovation that sparked a rebellion: How dissent exploded inside TMC
content: Trinamool Congress rebels have voiced dissent against Abhishek Banerjee, citing a post-election meeting where criticism of his leadership was forbidden. Expelled MLA Sandipan Saha stated legislators were instructed to applaud Banerjee despite electoral setbacks, highlighting growing resentment over concentrated power.

title: Ironic twist: Humayun offers Mamata route back to Bengal assembly
content: AJUP founder Humayun Kabir has offered Mamata Banerjee a path back to the West Bengal Assembly from his Rejinagar constituency. Kabir, who won both Nowda and Rejinagar, stated he would resign to support Banerjee's entry via a bypoll, citing her past role in his political career and acknowledging her current challenges.

title: What India nets revealed: Padikkal at No. 3; Test debut beckons for one
content: As India fine-tune preparations for the one-off Afghanistan Test, Devdutt Padikkal emerges as a strong contender for the vacant No. 3 spot, while one of Harsh Dubey or Manav Suthar looks set to make a Test debut.As India fine-tune preparations for the one-off Afghanistan Test, Devdutt Padikkal emerges as a strong contender for the vacant No. 3 spot, while one of Harsh Dubey or Manav Suthar looks set to make a Test debut.

title: US pharma giant is unhappy with Germany; CEO calls planned healthcare reform ‘terrible’
content: US drug maker Eli Lilly is cutting its investment in a German factory by half. This decision follows new healthcare cost-cutting laws in Germany. The company CEO called the reforms a bad signal for the industry. This move impacts Germany's industrial plans. The factory will now operate at reduced capacity with fewer jobs. Other companies are also reconsidering local investments.

title: Another 'nataka' brewing? CM-deputy pair stoke power tussle fears in Karnataka
content: G Parameshwara's appointment as deputy CM in Karnataka's DK Shivakumar government revives memories of past power tussles within the Congress. Previously, Parameshwara, as state party president, clashed with CM Siddaramaiah over party control. His current elevation is seen as a move to balance caste and factional interests, pacifying him after he vied for the CM post.

title: CEO of tech company arrested for supplying US-origin computer parts to Iran
content: Jamshid Ghomi, a dual US-Iranian national, has been arrested for allegedly violating US sanctions by shipping sophisticated US-origin technology to Iran.  He is accused of supplying networking, security, and encryption equipment to Iran's nuclear and military establishments, earning millions and laundering proceeds into his US accounts.

title: How FIFA is turning World Cup into an NBA, NFL-style made-for-TV event
content: The 2026 FIFA World Cup will feature mandatory three-minute hydration breaks midway through each half, effectively splitting matches into four quarters regardless of weather conditions. The change gives coaches extra tactical time and allows broadcasters to air commercials, adding over 10 hours of stoppage time across the tournament. While some coaches support the move, others fear it could disrupt football’s traditional flow and rhythm.

title: Delhi hotel fire: African couple undergoing IVF treatment found dead in each other's arms
content: An African couple undergoing IVF treatment in Delhi were found dead in each other's arms after a devastating fire at a Malviya Nagar hotel. They were among 21 victims, discovered in a bathroom where they sought refuge. The couple's tragic end highlighted their devotion, facing death together.

title: Bachelors vs families: Who is the ‘ideal resident’? Inside India's housing society divide
content: A viral social media post ignited a debate on housing society rules, highlighting suspicions towards bachelors and young couples. While societies need regulations for smooth functioning, many residents feel unfairly scrutinized and stereotyped, leading to a clash between community order and personal autonomy.

title: Google CEO Sundar Pichai sends a ‘thanks note’ to Warren Buffett’s Berkshire Hathaway
content: Alphabet has secured a massive $45 billion equity offering, with Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway leading with a $10 billion investment. This substantial capital infusion is earmarked for bolstering Google's AI compute infrastructure, a critical area identified by CEO Sundar Pichai. The company anticipates a total of $85 billion for this AI build-out, underscoring its strategic focus on artificial intelligence.

title: 'That's what's unusual': Cook makes fresh Sooryavanshi remark
content: Fifteen-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi has electrified the IPL 2026, smashing records with 776 runs and earning Orange Cap, MVP, and Emerging Player awards. His astonishing strike rate and six-hitting prowess have drawn praise from legends like Alastair Cook, who marvels at his instinctive batting.  The young sensation is now set for India A duty.

title: Lawyers making reels in court — Kerala high court bar association warns of misconduct action
content: Lawyers in Kerala are now on notice: filming social media reels within court premises could lead to professional misconduct charges. The Kerala High Court Advocates Association has warned that such actions violate ethical standards and could result in disciplinary action, including suspension or even disbarment, impacting a lawyer's career and reputation.

title: Rudram-II: How India's hypersonic missile uses enemy radars to destroy them
content: India is bolstering its aerial capabilities with the indigenous Rudram series of anti-radiation missiles, designed to neutralize enemy radar systems. These 'radar busters' are crucial for ensuring safe flight operations for Indian aircraft. Recent successful flight tests of the Rudram-II variant highlight India's commitment to advanced defense technology, complementing existing Russian-origin missiles.

title: Paying for 300 Mbps WiFi but getting only 50? Know how to file complaint and claim relief
content: Many Indian broadband users pay for high speeds but get less. This isn't just a service lapse; you can formally complain and seek compensation. Your recourse lies with TRAI for service quality violations or consumer courts for financial losses. Follow a three-step process: complain to your ISP, then TRAI, and finally, the consumer forum if unresolved.

title: ‘Ram Charan is lusting over her’: Fans upset over Janhvi’s portrayal in ‘Peddi’
content: Ram Charan and Janhvi Kapoor's 'Peddi' has sparked massive backlash within hours of its release. Fans are calling out the film for reducing Janhvi's character to an object of male desire, with many also questioning the censorship board's silence over her degrading portrayal on screen.

title: 'Profound humiliation' and 'sinister plot': Khamenei's message as ceasefire talks wobble
content: In a passionate address, Iran's Supreme Leader called for solidarity against the adversaries' schemes, emphasizing unwavering backing for the resistance front. He condemned Israel as a ‘cancerous growth’ on the regional landscape, suggesting its demise is imminent.

title: Connor McDavid trade rumors grow after Lauren Kyle's Montreal visit
content: For the second time in his career, Connor McDavid and the Edmonton Oilers are the subject of NHL trade reports, this time with the Montreal Canadiens. But later inside info suggested the speculation was being fuelled by Lauren Kyle McDavid’s visit to the city, not any hint of a major move involving the Edmonton Oilers captain.

title: Why Mourinho may not become Real Madrid's next manager despite Perez's confirmation
content: Jose Mourinho could return to Real Madrid, but the move depends on Florentino Perez winning the club’s upcoming presidential election. Perez has named Mourinho and Liverpool defender Ibrahima Konate as his priority signings if re-elected. He also dismissed speculation over Vinicius Junior’s future, insisting the Brazilian wants to stay and remains a key part of Madrid’s plans.

title: India expands ethanol map: 5,000 fuel stations planned by 2027 end
content: India is accelerating its ethanol drive, planning to establish 5,000 ethanol fuel stations by the end of 2027 to reduce fossil fuel imports. This initiative, alongside the launch of flex-fuel cars, aims to cut the country's $120 billion import bill and boost farmer income. Regulatory changes are also proposed to support higher ethanol blends and other alternative fuels.

title: ‘Perfect complementarity’: India, Venezuela deepen energy ties
content: India is bolstering energy ties with Venezuela, now its third-largest crude supplier, to diversify imports amidst West Asia disruptions. Discussions also explored broader economic cooperation in sectors like mining, pharmaceuticals, and agriculture, with Venezuela viewing India as a stable, long-term partner. High-level delegations are examining investment opportunities.

title: Forbidden anniversary: China blocks families from visiting graves of Tiananmen victims
content: China is intensifying its crackdown on any public remembrance of the 1989 Tiananmen Square events. Families of victims are being barred from visiting graves, and authorities are actively erasing the incident from public memory. This suppression escalates as Hong Kong also bans commemorations, while global reactions highlight ongoing censorship.

title: Five Eyes issues 'unprecedented' warning over China's aggressive spying campaign
content: Chinese intelligence operatives are reportedly using fake job offers on professional platforms to ensnare government and military personnel.  The Five Eyes alliance warns these tactics aim to extract sensitive information, with targets pressured to provide confidential details for financial rewards.  Western nations urge vigilance against these sophisticated espionage efforts.

title: Delhi weather disrupts air traffic: 13 flights go around, 11 diverted to other airports
content: Severe weather conditions, including rainfall and thunderstorms, disrupted flight operations at Delhi airport on Thursday. Thirteen incoming flights were forced to go around, with 11 diverted to other airports. A yellow alert remains in effect for Friday, forecasting continued light rain, thunderstorms, and strong winds.

title: ENG vs NZ: Why England players are wearing black armbands at Lord’s
content: England players wore black armbands and observed a moment of silence before the first Test against New Zealand at Lord’s to honour former England cricketers who have died since the team’s last home international. The pre-match tributes also marked 10 years since New Zealand great Martin Crowe’s passing, with his daughter Emma ringing the traditional five-minute bell.

title: BJP counters Rahul's 'economic tsunami' warning amid Iran war: 'Stop selling panic'
content: The BJP has strongly refuted Rahul Gandhi's "economic tsunami" warning, calling it fear-mongering. Party leader Amit Malviya highlighted India's economic resilience with strong indicators like rising E-way bills and FDI, asserting the government has implemented measures to protect citizens and businesses from global shocks, contrasting it with the UPA era's economic vulnerabilities.

title: Saif Ali Khan, Farhan Akhtar and others arrive for Pahlaj Nihalani's final rites
content: Veteran producer and former CBFC Chief Pahlaj Nihalani passed away at 76 due to liver cirrhosis. His funeral saw attendance from several industry colleagues, including Govinda, David Dhawan and his sons, and Shatrughan Sinha. Nihalani was known for producing films like 'Haathkadi' and launching Govinda's career.

title: Jewar airport corridor: Infrastructure project that could boost nearby property markets
content: A crucial 15.1 km corridor is nearing completion to seamlessly connect the upcoming Jewar International Airport with the Yamuna Expressway. This vital artery, part of the YEIDA master plan 2041, will significantly ease traffic flow for commuters from Noida, Greater Noida, and surrounding sectors.  The project promises smoother airport access and a faster alternative route, bolstering the region's exponential development.

title: 'Just want to quit tennis': Sabalenka after first French Open title ends - Watch
content: Aryna Sabalenka’s French Open title hopes ended after a dramatic quarterfinal collapse against Diana Shnaider. The world No. 1 led by a set and 4-1 in the second before losing 3-6, 7-5, 6-0. A devastated Sabalenka admitted she felt like quitting tennis after another major setback. Shnaider advanced to her first Grand Slam semifinal as Roland Garros continued to witness several shock upsets.

title: Who is Sarthak Sidhant? Class 12 student stirs storm with CBSE tender allegations
content: Sarthak Sidhant’s detailed blog on CBSE’s On-Screen Marking (OSM) system, based on an analysis of official tender documents linked to the digital evaluation of Class 12 answer sheets, has triggered wider questions on transparency and procurement processes. The issue has now reached political circles, with opposition leaders, including Congress leader Rahul Gandhi, sharing the blog and demanding accountability

title: How Lalit Modi convinced family to accept marriage to 10-years older Minal Sagrani
content: Lalit Modi's candid conversation reveals his decade-long love story with Minal Sagrani, a woman ten years his senior and divorced. Despite family opposition and societal judgment, their bond deepened through friendship and shared experiences, leading to a marriage that defied convention. Minal's passing in 2018 left Modi with profound grief and regret.

title: Mike Vrabel-Dianna Russini saga deepens as critics demand answers
content: NFL analyst Tony Farmer reignited controversy surrounding Mike Vrabel and Dianna Russini’s relationship, arguing that important questions remain unanswered. Farmer claimed the situation may have created potential conflicts affecting media coverage, NFL teams, and bettors. He criticized the lack of transparency about when the relationship began and noted that some Patriots reporters reportedly avoid discussing Russini publicly. Despite calls to move on, Farmer insists the public still lacks clarity on the matter.

title: For first time, Russia admits oil production has declined amid Ukraine's attacks on refineries
content: Russia has publicly acknowledged a dip in oil production due to unscheduled refinery repairs, marking the first such admission. This comes amid intensified Ukrainian drone attacks targeting Russian energy infrastructure, which have disrupted operations and pushed Moscow to increase crude exports. Despite these challenges, Russia aims to maximize exports and restore previous production levels.

title: 'I am hopeful': RCB's Krunal Pandya sets sights on India comeback after five years
content: Krunal, who played a key role in RCB's successful IPL 2026 title defence, contributed with both bat and ball during the season. He scored 226 runs at a strike rate of 145.80 and picked up 14 wickets at an economy rate of 8.41 as RCB won their second consecutive IPL title.

title: From bombers to oil terminals: How Zelensky brought the war to Putin's backyard
content: Ukraine is stepping up its game by launching strikes deep into Russian territory, aiming at vital oil and military assets to neutralize Moscow's combat advantages. The recent operations against air bases and munitions depots are meticulously planned to disrupt the flow of resources to Russian troops.

title: Sonia Gandhi backed Tharoor: Lalit Modi alleges Cong pressure during Kochi IPL row
content: Lalit Modi alleged that when he tried to stop the Kochi consortium from entering the IPL because of what he believed was an unfair equity arrangement involving Pushkar, the then United Progressive Alliance (UPA) government turned against him.

title: He came. He saw. He fled: Delhi hotel owner 'drove past' burning building
content: Hotel owner Lavkesh Bajaj reportedly fled a deadly south Delhi fire that claimed 21 lives, citing fear. Investigators are probing alleged building code violations and a lack of fire safety measures at the Flourish Stay B&B. The manager remains at large as authorities reconstruct the tragic events.

title: 'Dhurandhar 2' BO day 77: Ranveer Singh film misses Rs 1150 crore milestone
content: After 77 days and a record-shattering theatrical run, Aditya Dhar's espionage epic 'Dhurandhar 2: The Revenge' has minted a domestic net collection of Rs 1148.95 crore which is short of the Rs 1150 crore milestone by just Rs 1.05 crore. As the Ranveer Singh-led sequel transitions to its exclusive "Raw & Undekha" OTT premiere on JioHotstar today, June 4, 2026, the film's worldwide gross stands at a staggering Rs 1,812.82 crore.

title: Bengal: Bidhannagar mayor Krishna Chakraborty resigns amid TMC turmoil
content: Bidhannagar Municipal Corporation Mayor Krishna Chakraborty has resigned from her post, citing personal reasons. She submitted her resignation letter to the BMC commissioner on Thursday. Chakraborty stated she worked for the people during her tenure and will continue to do so as a councillor, expressing gratitude to the residents.

title: ITR filing FY 2025-26: Top 10 points to check before submitting tax return
content: Reporting income/assets etc. in the ITR should be matched with Annual Information Statement (AIS). As through system checks and reconciliation runs, using technology for ITR processing might trigger even small gaps, errors or incorrect claims and may lead to prompt questions.

title: Akshay Kumar sells two flats in Borivali for Rs 7.1 crore: Report
content: Akshay Kumar has sold two residential units in Oberoi Sky City, Borivali (East), Mumbai, for a total of ₹7.1 crore. The larger apartment, spanning 1,101 sq ft, fetched ₹5.75 crore, while the smaller 252 sq ft unit sold for ₹1.35 crore. Both properties were acquired by Suvarna Rupeshkumar Sakpal.

title: DMK to boycott June 8 INDIA bloc meeting over Congress ‘betrayal’
content: The Congress, which had won five seats as part of the DMK-led Secular Progressive Alliance, had post-polls unilaterally snapped ties with the Dravidian party and joined the TVK-led coalition government.

title: Central Minister expresses anguish over Yadgir’s low per capita income, human development index
content: Nirmala Sitharaman was addressing a gathering after inaugurating a Farmers Training and Common Facility Centre in Baddepalli village

title: CBI books man for allegedly posing as PMO official
content: Case registered after PMO complaint over wedding invitation card; accused allegedly sought “VIP darshan” arrangements at temples in Tamil Nadu

title: Under-construction five-storey building collapses in Idukki
content: No casualties reported. Peerumade panchayat secretary says building owner had obtained a valid construction permit

title: Bangladesh says it foiled multiple attempts by India to force people into the country
content: Dhaka has ⁠repeatedly said anyone identified as a Bangladeshi national should be returned through ⁠formal legal and diplomatic channels rather than being driven across the border

title: Cockroach Janta Party’s rise reflects people’s  frustration with the BJP: Hooda
content: The Congress, the former Haryana CM says, has been consistently raising people-centric issues in its role as the main Opposition party and would continue to do so, while the CJP was in its formative stage

title: Watch: Southwest monsoon reaches Kerala, IMD issues yellow alert for Delhi
content: The southwest monsoon set in over Kerala on Thursday (June 4, 2026), the India Meteorological Department announced. This is three days later than the normal onset date of June 1, and four days behind the date the department had forecast.

title: Take steps to strengthen Baddepalli agro-processing CFC, says Nirmala Sitharaman
content: The Common Facility Centre has been established through funds released under the MP-LAD scheme and also financial support from NABARD

title: 1,260 children of migrant labourers identified for pulse polio drive in Kozhikode
content: 27 mobile units to be deployed near residential premises, labour camps and other areas frequented by migrant labourers

title: Have not received any resignation letter from Annamalai: Nainar Nagenthran
content: Only the media is magnifying this issue and making a mountain out of a molehill, says T.N. BJP president

title: Pakistan accuses India of weaponising water after treaty suspension
content: Pakistan's Foreign Ministry spokesperson Tahir Andrabi said that New Delhi had not consulted Islamabad on the two Chenab River projects that he said would undermine the Indus Water Treaty

title: Ahmedabad water contamination: 7 remain hospitalised; Union Minister Shah reviews situation
content: Health Department teams visited all housing societies where diarrhoea and vomiting cases were reported and conducted house-to-house surveys, while chlorine tablets were also distributed for water purification

title: Harassment charges: two Psychiatry doctors at Kozhikode MCH transferred
content: This follows the recommendations proposed by a three-member panel appointed by the Directorate of Medical Education to inquire into the allegations levelled by the 12 PG students

title: Japanese robotics startup searching for manufacturing partners in India
content: Jinki-Ittai robots are used in Japan for maintenance of railway lines and other areas where human lives are at risk; startup is specifically exploring opportunities in India in foundries and railways

title: Pawan Kalyan’s allegations against YSR lack evidence, asserts K.V.P. Ramachandra Rao
content: During 2005-2006, there is no political necessity for the former Chief Minister to target Pawan Kalyan, argues the senior Congress leader

title: Annamalai quits BJP, to start new political ‘movement’
content: Sources told The Hindu that Mr. Annamalai submitted his resignation letter to the party leadership on June 2, 2026

title: Eviction of Malayidamthuruth Dalit families: Kerala HC grants govt two more weeks to resolve impasse
content: HC restrains Perumbavur munsiff court from passing orders till June 16 on the families’ eviction from 2.5 acres of land, the ownership of which has been claimed by a private individual

title: Revenue department evicts encroachment on government land in Munnar
content: Nearly 20 families had encroached upon government Puramboke land in M.G. Colony and erected temporary shedsIDUKKI

title: Kuki organisation in Manipur opposes SIR of electoral rolls
content: Kuki Inpi Manipur doubts the fairness of the exercise as 59,000 Kuki-Zo people continue to remain displaced

title: Man arrested over threat to Kona villagers with country-made gun
content: The Bandar Taluka police arrest G. Nageswara Rao of Kona village in Krishna district for allegedly threatening residents with a country-made gun and seize the weapon

title: 2020 Delhi riots: Court defers verdict in IB officer murder case for June 11
content: Additional Sessions Judge Praveen Singh was hearing the case against 11 accused, including former AAP councillor Tahir Hussain, and deferred the pronouncement of his judgment

title: ‘Use Andhra Pradesh as your launch pad’: Lokesh asks Russian firms to orbit Sriharikota
content: At SPIEF 2026, Nara Lokesh pitches Andhra Pradesh’s spaceport, Space Policy 4.0 and a planned $4-billion space ecosystem to Russian investors

title: RBI issues compounding order for FEMA violations in Myntra Designs case
content: The order, involving a one-time payment of ₹2.88 lakh, has been passed following a ‘no objection’ from the ED

title: First Cabinet meeting of TVK-led Tamil Nadu government to focus on Tasmac reforms, investment promotion
content: The State government is planning a major administrative overhaul of Tasmac operations, from the procurement stage to the retail sale of liquor

title: Udhayanidhi Stalin urges CM Vijay to block surrender of 152 super-specialty medical seats to national quota
content: He alleged that the situation arose because the current TVK-led government failed to present proper arguments before the Supreme Court recently

title: Southwest monsoon arrives in Karnataka: IMD
content: The IMD has already stated that the 2026 southwest monsoon seasonal rainfall (June to September) across Karnataka is expected to be below normal in most parts of the State.

title: Monsoon 2026 LIVE: Southwest monsoon arrives in Kerala, says IMD
content: The India Meteorological Department issues orange alert for six districts as heavy rains hit parts of Kerala

title: White Paper puts Kerala’s liabilities at ₹5.07-lakh crore, CM says fiscal structure under ‘serious’ strain
content: Salaries, pensions, and interest payments consumed 77.6% of the revenue receipts in 2025-26; a major revamp of KIIFB recommended; urgent reforms sought in KSEB, KSRTC, and KWA; State’s capital expenditure at 1.3% of the GSDP is “one of the lowest” among Indian States

title: Tiruchi Surya arrested for posting defamatory, derogatory video targeting woman on YouTube
content: The Social Media Investigation Wing of the CCB Cyber Crime Police Station took action following a complaint lodged by a Chennai-based woman

title: Cockroach Janata Party attacks govt. on JEE data exposure, presses for Education Minister’s sacking
content: CJP spokesperson Ashutosh Ranka, in a press conference, sought to dispel speculation that the CJP's proposed June 6 agitation could turn violent and reiterated its commitment to peaceful protest

title: Delhi HC protects personality rights of actor Naga Chaitanya
content: Justice Jyoti Singh, in an interim order, restrained third parties from selling merchandise bearing Mr. Chaitanya’s name and image without consent, and directed the removal of the objectionable content from online platforms

title: Tamil Nadu government appoints 34 Ministers in-charge of all 38 districts
content: The 34 Ministers would coordinate with District Collectors and other district-level officials to expedite ongoing projects, ensure the delivery of welfare schemes, and carry out relief work during disasters.

title: Cuddalore government school toppers now get to learn scuba diving
content: The camp, which began last week at Temple Adventures, has attracted 27 boys who emerged with flying colours in their Plus Two exams

title: Rahul Gandhi warns of ‘institutional revolt’ in Modi government
content: An unprecedented ‘economic tsunami’ was set to hit India, the Modi government’s control over institutions had collapsed; it might ‘impose something like an Emergency to suppress public pressure’, says the Congress leader

title: Omalur TVK candidate claims cash distribution by party nominee in neighbouring constituency led to his defeat
content: At a meeting of TVK functionaries held in Omalur in May, R.V. Adhiyamaan outlined what he believed were the reasons for his defeat in the segment

title: PM Modi holds talks with Venezuela’s acting President
content: The two sides are looking at expanding energy cooperation, as New Delhi seeks to diversify its crude oil procurement amid supply disruptions from the West Asia crisis

title: Construction worker trampled to death by elephant in Kadambur hills
content: R. Basuvaraj was travelling on a two-wheeler with his friend, Sivakumar, from Kadambur to Iruttipalayam along a forest road around 9 p.m. When they reached the 12th Mile stretch, a wild elephant that was crossing the road attacked Basuvaraj, killing him on the spot

title: From Schitt's Creek to Desi Bling: Is cringe-binge actually about self-care?
content: While not jumping with joy seeing every dog that comes her way (to the bewilderment of the dog owner and the dog), Haimanti fantasizes about fantasy books or classics to read and re-read. That could be the gist of it all, except for the aroma of biryani that beckons; or that idea that's stuck in the head and refuses to go till it's penned down. Read More

title: Wipro Ex-Staff Alleges Woman Boss Of "Forced Conversion", Company Responds
content: A Wipro spokesperson in a statement said they have been cooperating with the authorities and provided all documents and information with the Pune Police

title: One Jump Saved His Life: Ludhiana Man's Fight For Survival After Delhi Fire
content: As the deadly hotel fire, which claimed the lives of atleast 21 people, continues to reveal its human cost, families from across the country are struggling to come to terms with a loss that has changed their lives forever.

title: Video: Rogue Elephant 'Padayappa' Strikes Again In Kerala, Damages Car
content: The notorious wild tusker Padayappa has struck again in Kerala's Munnar, damaging a car parked in the courtyard of a house at Nallathanni Estate and renewing concerns over recurring human-elephant conflict in the region.

title: Trinamool Has One More Split Ahead, In Parliament: Expelled Leader To NDTV
content: Twenty MLAs of the Trinamool, Kabir said, have already spoken to the Speaker regarding Abhishek Banerjee's removal.

title: Political Commentator Trichy Surya Arrested Over Obscene Remarks In Chennai
content: Chennai Police have arrested political commentator Trichy Surya for allegedly making vulgar and sexually explicit remarks about racer and BJP functionary Alisha Abdullah and her newborn twins during a conversation aired on a YouTube channel.

title: Can 'Black Gold' Power India's Future? 'Dirty Coal' Finds New Uses
content: India's annual coal demand is already around 1 billion tonnes and is expected to rise further by 2047. At the same time, the country has committed to achieving net zero emissions by 2070

title: AAP Government Launches Large-Scale Menstrual Health Education Drive In Punjab Schools
content: More than 3.4 lakh girls in government schools will be covered under the new curriculum introduced across Punjab

title: Plague On Both Your Houses: Bengal Minister On Divided Trinamool
content: "Let them squabble. Let them self-destruct. I don't expect that we should have to choose between these," Swapan Dasgupta said.

title: PM Modi Holds Talks With Venezuela Acting President, Discusses Bilateral Ties
content: Earlier, External Affairs Minister S. Jaishankar met Delcy Rodriguez and reaffirmed India's commitment to strengthening ties with the South American nation.

title: Amid Annamalai In-Or-Out Buzz, BJP's 'No Resignation' Clarification
content: Tamil Nadu BJP chief Nainar Nagendran played down reports that K Annamalai will launch a people's movement that will culminate in a political party.

title: 13 Foreigners Among 15 Who Survived Delhi Hotel Fire Still Under Treatment
content: Malviya Nagar fire: Many of those staying at the hotel were foreign medical tourists or attendants of patients undergoing treatment at nearby hospitals.

title: JEE Aspirant Found Hanging In Rented Room In Kota, Suicide Suspected
content: Around 10.45 pm on Wednesday, other students residing at the PG discovered the body and informed police.

title: 'No Revenue Inflation': Rajesh Exports Rejects Rs 15 Lakh Crore Scam Charge
content: According to SEBI, the mismatch amounted to around Rs 15.15 lakh crore, making it one of the biggest alleged cases of revenue misrepresentation.

title: Chintan Research Foundation Organises Policy Dialogue On Small Modular Reactors
content: The Chintan Research Foundation and Finovista dialogue brought together policymakers, industry leaders, and experts to discuss the role of small modular reactors in supporting India's energy transition and strengthening critical sectors.

title: Centre Preps Delimitation Push, Eyes New Bill Before 2029 Elections
content: The delimitation exercise could pave the way for the first major redrawing of parliamentary constituencies in decades and reopen a debate that has long divided India's political landscape

title: "Ideology With Which Party Was Formed Does Not Exist": Expelled Trinamool MLA
content: The crisis in Trinamool reached a breakpoint on Wednesday when 58 rebel MLAs elected expelled leader Ritabrata Banerjee as Leader of Opposition, wresting control of Trinamool's legislature party.

title: Opinion: Opinion | Forget Coding. The World's Hottest Jobs Now Belong To Plumbers, Electricians
content: As many as 85% of companies in blue-collar industries say they have unfilled vacancies - even as world's most talented coders and engineers are going unemployed.

content: Stock Market, Sensex, Share Market, Nifty LIVE Updates: Oil prices eased as Israel and Lebanon's ceasefire agreement boosted hopes for US-Iran deal.

title: Tamil Nadu polls fallout: DMK to boycott INDIA bloc meet on June 8
content: DMK announced that it will boycott the INDIA bloc meeting to be held in Delhi on June 8 . the decision comes after Congress severed its ties with DMk following the assembly election debacle . DMKO issued a statement saying that DM K will not participate in the meeting to give respect to the sentiments of its cadres .
